name: Yamagata Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the blue skies and waters of Yamagata
the snows of the Dewa mountains

description: A blue field with a white emblem at the centre formed from three peaks (with stepped tops) above a small triangular base, stylising both the kanji 'yama' (mountain) and Yamagata's three sacred peaks.
history: Yamagata's prefectural symbol was adopted on 8 August 1963 and the flag bearing it on a blue field is in use from the same date.

features.symbolism: the blue of clear skies above the Mogami River and the Sea of Japan

features.role: stylised triple-mountain emblem combining the three sacred peaks of Dewa Sanzan (Mount Haguro, Mount Gassan, Mount Yudono) with the kanji 'yama' (山, mountain) for Yamagata
features.symbolism: Yamagata's three sacred mountains (Dewa Sanzan) and the kanji 'yama' (山) for the prefecture's name; the form rests on a triangular base evoking the rivers of the prefecture.
